conjecture: On a real external OpenObserve store (docker-compose public.ecr.aws/zinclabs/openobserve:v0.14.7, separate process/container), the shipped ooptdd engine catches silent evidence loss (catch rate 1.0), manufactures no false RED under declared ingestion lag (false-RED rate 0.0), stays inconclusive-honest under store outage (honesty rate 1.0), and restores to present after auth/store recovery (restore rate 1.0) — across 20 seeded repetitions per scenario, with valid candidate/preregistration/environment/registration bindings and a store identity distinct from MemoryBackend.
registration_mechanism: git commit in gj3447/lakatotree before candidate measurement
prior_measurement_boundary: All ooptdd measurements at or before candidate db5737d02b64f45b8b47d7609721f1f168e21eba are Tier-0 mechanics-only or exploratory. Tier-0 results, demo scripts (demo_green/demo_silent_401/demo_inconclusive), and CI openobserve-demos jobs are not Tier-1 evidence and carry no external-arrival claim weight.

scenarios.fault: shipper suppresses the emit of one required event
receipts: independent store query shows required event missing

scenarios.fault: fire-and-forget shipper with wrong password swallows HTTP 401
ingest proxy holds POSTs for T ms, T <= declared query_visibility_delay_ms (5000)
scenarios.expected: present; zero false RED; arrival stamp shows visibility extension
inconclusive with exit-2 semantics; JUnit renders skipped/inconclusive, never failure

scenarios.fault: 401 negative first, then auth fixed and same spec replayed

kill_condition: Reject the conjecture if any of the four rates misses its target; if any loss trial returns green (fake green); if any outage trial returns absent instead of inconclusive or renders as a JUnit failure; if any binding (candidate head, preregistration sha, environment, registration repository) is invalid; or if the store identity equals MemoryBackend.
negative_controls: run with a dirty candidate worktree must be refused by the lock validator
run with a mismatched preregistration sha must be refused
an outage trial must not be recorded as absent
a suppressed-event trial must never be recorded as present
restored run must follow, never precede, its negative
honesty_boundaries: A Tier-1 pass authorizes only a narrow claim: OpenObserve v0.14.7 (compose), five fixed fault scenarios, 20-repetition seeded panel, candidate db5737d — not production-distribution generalization, not other backends.
Repetitions share invariant mechanics; pass_hat_k and Wilson intervals describe the fixed panel, not a population.
Sentences forbidden before this measurement (silent loss 100% in production / external arrival efficacy verified / battle-tested) remain forbidden outside these qualifiers.

Local OpenObserve (single binary v0.14.7, same version as the reference compose image, separate process on port 5081, throwaway data dir) is the Tier-1 judge of record; the shared dgx store is not used, avoiding cross-project pollution and credential-in-artifact risk. Amended 2026-07-24T17:20Z: Docker Desktop unavailable on the measurement host (dangling CLI symlink) — compose replaced by the equivalent single binary.
